rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANTs THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION, TRAVELERS CASUALTY AND SURETY COMPANY OF AMERICA & CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer on the grounds that the Cross-Complaint fails to state facts sufficient is sustained with leave to amend. SD Deacon is given leave to amend to allege who made the fraudulent statements or concealed facts, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, and when it was said or written. The demurrer is overruled on all other grounds. The Stipulation re: Binding Arbitration did not deprive this Court of subject matter jurisdiction because it did not stay this case and no party moved to stay the case pending arbitration. The Cross-Complaint is not barred by CCP § 426.30 because SD Deacon has not asserted the X-C in "any other action" and SD Deacon did not waive its claims by failing to allege them as an affirmative in the answer to the Metropolitan Association's Cross-Complaint. SD Deacon was permitted to file the Cross-Complaint under CCP § 428.10(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SUSAN Y F LEE VS. WAH-BUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents. Granted. On January 28, 2013, the court issued an order granting Defendants' Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Independent Medical Examination. Within 10 days, Plaintiff shall provide a written authorization on Kaiser's form permitting Kaiser to produce Plaintiff's MOCA and PHQ-9 test results to Defendants' counsel. Consistent with the court's previous order issued on January 28, 2013, the information provided by Kaiser shall be kept confidential and may not be disclosed to third persons except counsel for the parties and as required for the conduct of the litigation. Said information may not be disclosed to Plaintiff's current employer or any future employer, or any insurer, whether a health insurer, life insurer, or liability insurer unless specifically authorized by Plaintiff. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. RANDHIR SINGH GOLEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn Fo Rterminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: GREEN HORIZON MANUFACTURING LLC, VS. DAN CHAON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice O Fmotion And Otion To Dismiss Or Stay Matter For Forum On Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTs TIM IRISH, MERIDIAN WORKING CAPITAL, LLC & MERIDIAN PO FINANCE, LLC's motion To Dismiss Or Stay Matter For Forum On Conveniens. The motion is granted and the Court stays the action. Plaintiff should have commenced this action in Arizona pursuant to pars. 1.6 and 21.6 of the contract. The contract is not ambiguous and Plaintiff's interpretation of par. 21.6 is unreasonable. The language regarding the CEO's office describes the federal district court in Arizona where a party should commence an action. The CEO language does not determine the "controlling state." The entire action, including the fraud cause of action, is stayed. The fraud claim is directly tied to the contract. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate that enforcement of the mandatory forum selection clause would be unreasonable under the present circumstances. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JAMES POWELL VS. FOP DEVELOPMENT GROUP LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Fop Development Group Llc, Green Power Group Llc, Patrick Mckay, Karrie Bercik, Gary Taormina And Wayne Schafnit'S Hearing Re: Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Litigation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 16, Defendants Fop Development Group Llc, Green Power Group Llc, Patrick Mckay, Karrie Bercik, Gary Taormina And Wayne Schafnit'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Litigation. Granted in its entirety. Petitioners have met their burden to show that a written agreement to arbitrate exists among the parties. (CCP § 1281.2.) Although the Operating Agreement is not attached to the FOP Purchase and Subscription Agreement, Mr. Powell specifically represented and warranted that he had read and understood the Operating Agreement. (Bercik Decl., filed February 7, 2013, Exhibit A, 6(a)) Mr. Powell agreed to be bound by the terms of the Operating Agreement (Id. at 2(a).) The FOP Purchase and Subscription Agreement was executed on April 21, 2009. (Petition, Bercik Decl., Exhibit A, p. 26.) The Second Amended Operating Agreement was executed on February 11, 2008. (Id. at Exhibit B, BATES no. KB000071.) The Second Amended Operating Agreement was in effect at the time Mr. Powell signed the FOP Purchase and Subscription Agreement. (Bercik Decl., filed February 7, 2013, 4.) Unlike Adajar v. RWR Homes, Inc. (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 563, 569-70, the terms of the arbitration agreement can be determined. Mr. Powell does not oppose CGC's petition to arbitrate. (Opposition, p. 11:15-22.) Mr. Powell does not argue that the arbitration agreements are unconscionable. The action is stayed pending arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO AMADO VAZ VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY & CLIFFORD GILMORE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Hearing required. The Court is inclined to rule as follows: Granted in part and denied in part. The motion to strike all references to malice, oppression, and fraud is denied. These claims are not subject to a heightened pleading standard. The motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is denied. The claim is sufficiently pled. The motion to strike all prayers for attorney's fees is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff is representing himself and not a licensed attorney. The motion to strike the portions of the prayers for relief that are not a demand for judgment is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 425.10 does not require otherwise. The motion to strike paragraph (c) is granted as to the statement "damages for unpaid wages, including regular and overtime wages" without leave to amend. The complaint has alleged no facts indicating unpaid wages. The motion to strike paragraph (f) of the prayer is granted without leave to amend. The complaint has alleged no facts indicating that plaintiff was denied access to any records. The motion to strike paragraph (g) of the prayer is granted with leave to amend in light of the Court's ruling on the related cause of action. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting a present controversy. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO AMADO VAZ VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTs TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY & CLIFFORD GILMORE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Hearing required. The Court is inclined to rule as follows: Overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first and second causes of action for failure to exhaust administrative remedies is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts showing that he has obtained a right to sue letter from the Department of Fair Housing and Employment for these claims. The demurrer to the first, second, and third causes of action as to the individual defendants Shaw, Tekkey, and Gilmore is sustained without leave to amend. Only employers are liable for these causes of action. The demurrer to the first cause of action for failure to state a claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to allege sufficient facts for a claim of race or national origin discrimination. The demurrer to the third cause of action for failure to state a claim is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim for wrongful discharge based on disability discrimination or retaliation. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. All of the conduct giving rise to the claim stem from the employment relationship and fall within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Worker's Compensation Appeals Board. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting unfair business practices. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting a present controversy. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: NITOSHA STROTHER VS. SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT, AKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the negligence causes of action because those are common law claims. See Hoff v. Vacaville Unified School Dist., (1998) 19 Cal.4th 925, 942. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the premises liability causes of action. Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead claims for dangerous condition of public property per California Government Code Section 835. Plaintiff also has leave to amend to plead any other basis for liability. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEVIN SIMES, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HERSH & HERSH, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 21, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTs HERSH & HERSH, NANCY HERSH & MARK BURTON's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted as to the second, third and fifth causes of action and denied as to the first and fourth causes of action. The second, third and fifth causes of action arise from protected activity. On the facts of this case, the commercial speech exception in CCP §425.17 clearly does not apply. (See Taheri Law Group v. Evans (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 482, 489-492.) Plaintiff has failed to show a probability of prevailing because the claims are barred by the litigation privilege. (See Cal. Civ. Code § 47(b).) The first and fourth causes of action do not arise from protected activity because the gravamen of those claims is not the protected speech. Plaintiff's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
